hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; [[ "$f" =~ \.(ts|tsx)$ ]] && bunx eslint --fix "$f" 2>/dev/null || true; } 2>/dev/null || true

hooks.prompt:
Review this H2O Church web app development session for learnable patterns:
1. Run: git -C /Users/danluk/projects/h2ochurch log --oneline origin/main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | head -10
2. Run: git -C /Users/danluk/projects/h2ochurch diff origin/main...HEAD --stat 2>/dev/null | head -30
3. Read: /Users/danluk/.claude/projects/-Users-danluk-projects-h2ochurch/memory/MEMORY.md

Identify NON-OBVIOUS learnings (codebase patterns, API quirks, performance pitfalls, surprising behaviors, deployment gotchas) not already in memory. If found, write them as memory files at /Users/danluk/.claude/projects/-Users-danluk-projects-h2ochurch/memory/ using frontmatter format (---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\nmetadata:\n  type: feedback|project|reference\n---\n<body with Why: and How to apply: lines>), then add pointer lines to MEMORY.md.

Be highly selective — only save what prevents a real mistake or saves significant time in future sessions. If nothing genuinely new, output 'No new learnings this session.'
